stories.headline: Lionel Messi is a Spider-Man fan: Football legend tracks down Tom Holland's Peter Parker
stories.what: Football legend Lionel Messi has revealed himself to be a Spider-Man fan, going so far as to track down Tom Holland's portrayal of Peter Parker. The story captures a charming intersection of global sporting royalty and pop-culture fandom, with Messi — one of the most celebrated athletes in history — expressing enthusiasm for the Marvel superhero franchise. The anecdote is a rare glimpse into the personal tastes of an athlete whose public persona is almost entirely defined by football.
stories.lens: Lionel Messi's Spider-Man fandom and his personal interest in Tom Holland's Peter Parker is the kind of pop-culture crossover that delights Indian fans who follow both cricket-adjacent global sport and Marvel films.
stories.article:
Lionel Messi, the Argentine football legend and one of the most recognisable human beings on the planet, has a secret identity of his own — he is a devoted Spider-Man fan, and he made sure to track down Tom Holland's version of Peter Parker.

The story, reported by The Indian Express, is the kind of detail that humanises a global icon in an unexpected way. Messi, whose public persona is almost entirely about football, revealing a genuine enthusiasm for one of cinema's most beloved superhero franchises is both surprising and immediately endearing.

Tom Holland has played Peter Parker — the teenage incarnation of Spider-Man in the Marvel Cinematic Universe — across multiple films, most recently in Spider-Man: No Way Home, which became one of the highest-grossing films in history. That Messi specifically sought out Holland's portrayal rather than the broader franchise speaks to a genuine connection with the character as Holland has portrayed him.

For Indian audiences, who are among the most passionate consumers of both international football and Marvel content, the crossover is a moment of pure delight. It also underlines the extraordinary global reach of the MCU — a franchise that counts among its fans not just millions of ordinary moviegoers, but the man many consider the greatest footballer of all time.
key_facts: Lionel Messi is a Spider-Man fan
Messi tracked down Tom Holland's Peter Parker
Tom Holland plays Spider-Man in the Marvel Cinematic Universe

stories.headline: Indian entertainment promoter Deepak Choudhary faces travel ban and asset freeze in Dubai over alleged Rs 7.7 crore dispute
stories.what: Indian entertainment promoter Deepak Choudhary has been hit with a travel ban and asset freeze by Dubai authorities over allegations involving Rs 7.7 crore. The legal action, taken in the UAE, marks a significant escalation in what appears to be a financial dispute tied to his entertainment promotion business. The case puts a spotlight on the legal risks faced by Indian promoters operating in overseas markets.
stories.lens: An Rs 7.7 crore dispute landing a prominent Indian entertainment promoter under a UAE travel ban is a sharp reminder of the legal exposure Indian showbiz operators face when conducting business abroad.
stories.article:
Indian entertainment promoter Deepak Choudhary is facing a travel ban and asset freeze in Dubai over an alleged financial dispute worth Rs 7.7 crore, in a development that has sent shockwaves through the event promotion industry.

Dubai authorities imposed the restrictions as part of legal proceedings tied to the alleged dues. A travel ban prevents Choudhary from leaving the UAE, while the asset freeze locks down his financial holdings there — two of the most severe pre-judgment remedies available under UAE law.

Choudhary is known in Indian entertainment circles as a promoter involved in large-scale events and concerts, often bridging Bollywood and international acts for audiences in the Gulf region. The nature of the underlying dispute — who the alleged creditor is and the circumstances of the Rs 7.7 crore claim — has not been fully detailed in available reports, but the scale of the legal action suggests a serious commercial disagreement.

The case underlines the complex legal terrain that Indian promoters navigate when operating in the Gulf, where civil and commercial disputes can result in swift travel bans with little prior warning. For India's event promotion ecosystem, which relies heavily on Gulf markets and the large Indian diaspora there, the episode serves as a cautionary tale about contractual and financial due diligence.
key_facts: Deepak Choudhary is an Indian entertainment promoter
Dubai authorities imposed a travel ban and asset freeze
Alleged dispute involves Rs 7.7 crore

stories.headline: Raveena Tandon compares Welcome 3 to Jim Carrey and Adam Sandler films: 'You expect logic?'
stories.what: Actor Raveena Tandon has defended Welcome 3 by comparing it to the brand of comedy films headlined by Hollywood stars Jim Carrey and Adam Sandler, asking rhetorically whether audiences expect logic from such entertainers. Her comments come as the film prepares for release amid the usual scrutiny over whether the Welcome franchise can sustain its comic energy. Tandon's framing positions Welcome 3 squarely as a no-apologies comedy spectacle rather than a narratively tight film.
stories.lens: Raveena Tandon's defence of Welcome 3 as pure comedy spectacle — invoking Jim Carrey and Adam Sandler — tells audiences exactly what they're in for, and sets the tone for how the film's makers want it to be received.
stories.article:
Raveena Tandon is not asking audiences to bring their critical faculties to Welcome 3 — she is asking them to leave logic at the door, much as they would for a Jim Carrey or Adam Sandler film.

In a candid interview, Tandon drew the comparison explicitly, pointing out that the beloved Hollywood comedy stars have built entire careers on absurdist, illogical humour that audiences adore precisely because it makes no pretence at realism. 'You expect logic?' she asked, making clear that Welcome 3 belongs in that tradition of big, broad, unapologetic entertainment.

The Welcome franchise has always occupied a specific lane in Bollywood — ensemble comedies built around outlandish gangster characters and escalating comic set-pieces, with little interest in plot coherence. The third instalment continues that tradition, with a large cast and the same chaotic energy that made the original a cult favourite.

Tandon's comments are likely to resonate with fans of the franchise who turn up for the laughs rather than the story. Whether the comparison to Carrey and Sandler holds up on screen will depend on execution, but as a marketing pitch, it is disarmingly honest — and probably the smartest way to set expectations for a film that has no intention of being anything other than a good time.
key_facts: Raveena Tandon compares Welcome 3 to Jim Carrey and Adam Sandler comedies
She dismisses expectations of logic from the film
Welcome 3 is the third instalment in the Welcome franchise
stories.image_credit: Ministry of Information and Broadcasting / Wikimedia Commons

stories.headline: 'Main Vaapas Aaunga is an act of rebellion,' says Mahesh Bhatt, compares it to Arth and Zakhm
stories.what: Filmmaker Mahesh Bhatt has described his upcoming project Main Vaapas Aaunga as 'an act of rebellion', drawing a direct comparison to two of his most celebrated and intensely personal films — Arth and Zakhm. The declaration signals that Bhatt views this new film as a return to the raw, confessional style of storytelling that defined his finest work. Coming from a filmmaker who has largely stepped back from directing, the statement has generated considerable buzz in Bollywood.
stories.lens: Mahesh Bhatt invoking Arth and Zakhm — two films considered among Hindi cinema's most searingly personal — sets unusually high expectations for Main Vaapas Aaunga and marks a significant creative comeback.
stories.article:
Mahesh Bhatt has staked out bold creative ground for his new film Main Vaapas Aaunga, calling it 'an act of rebellion' and placing it in the same lineage as Arth and Zakhm — the two films widely regarded as the most brutally honest of his career.

Bhatt made these remarks in an interview, framing the project not as a commercial venture but as a deeply personal statement. His comparison to Arth, the 1982 film that drew from his own tumultuous personal life, and Zakhm, the 1997 film rooted in his experience of religious identity and his mother's death, suggests Main Vaapas Aaunga will carry a similarly autobiographical or emotionally unguarded quality.

The filmmaker, who has not directed a film in decades, has been associated with the project in a capacity that underlines his renewed creative engagement. The word 'rebellion' is pointed: it implies a conscious pushback against the dominant trends of mainstream Bollywood — spectacle, franchise films, and algorithm-driven content.

For audiences who grew up on Bhatt's 1980s and 1990s output, the comparison carries real weight. Arth and Zakhm were films that made viewers uncomfortable in the best possible way, refusing easy resolution. Whether Main Vaapas Aaunga can recapture that spirit remains to be seen, but Bhatt's framing of the film has already made it one of the more anticipated artistic conversations in Hindi cinema.
key_facts: Mahesh Bhatt calls Main Vaapas Aaunga 'an act of rebellion'
He compares it to his films Arth and Zakhm
Signals a return to personal, confessional filmmaking

stories.headline: Director Ahmed Khan reveals why Sanjay Dutt quit Welcome To The Jungle after starting shoot
stories.what: Director Ahmed Khan has publicly revealed the reason behind Sanjay Dutt's departure from Welcome To The Jungle, a film Dutt had actually begun shooting before exiting the project. The disclosure ends months of speculation about why one of the film's marquee stars walked away mid-production, a rare and disruptive event in Bollywood filmmaking. The details of Khan's explanation shed light on the behind-the-scenes dynamics of a production that was already generating significant industry attention.
stories.lens: A director publicly explaining why a top star like Sanjay Dutt left a major production mid-shoot is unusually candid for Bollywood — and the real reason matters to anyone tracking how big Hindi films actually get made.
stories.article:
Director Ahmed Khan has broken his silence on one of Bollywood's more talked-about production surprises — Sanjay Dutt's exit from Welcome To The Jungle after the actor had already commenced shooting on the film.

Khan's disclosure, made in an interview covered by The Indian Express, puts to rest speculation that had swirled around the departure. Dutt leaving a film after production has begun is a logistically and financially significant event, requiring the production to recast, reschedule, and in some cases reshoot footage already completed — making the director's explanation an important piece of the story.

Welcome To The Jungle, a large-ensemble Bollywood comedy, attracted attention partly because of its sprawling cast and its connection to the Welcome franchise. Dutt's involvement had been considered a significant addition, making his exit all the more notable when it emerged.

The director's willingness to speak openly about the circumstances reflects a broader shift in how Bollywood productions are discussed publicly — with filmmakers increasingly willing to address production setbacks rather than maintain a wall of silence. For audiences and industry watchers alike, the account provides rare visibility into the pressures and negotiations that shape what ultimately reaches the screen.
key_facts: Sanjay Dutt quit Welcome To The Jungle after starting the shoot
Director Ahmed Khan has revealed the reason for Dutt's departure
Welcome To The Jungle is a large-ensemble Bollywood comedy
